LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MADISON ONE ACME, INC.'S MOTION TO AMEND THE 2008 CONSENT JUDGMENT. A R G U E D. THE COURT FINDS THAT 1717(B)(2) APPLIES, THAT THIS IS A POST-JUDGMENT MATTER. SO IT IS, EFFECTIVELY, THE EQUIVALENT OF AN ACTION. THE MOTION HAS BEEN WITHDRAWN. THERE IS NO PREVAILING PARTY PER THAT CODE SECTION. HOWEVER, IF THE MOTION IS FILED AGAIN AND THE PLAINTIFF WISHES, THE COURT IS NOT PRECLUDING THE PLAINTIFF FROM SEEKING FEES FOR THIS MOTION, AS WELL AS THE SUBSEQUENT MOTION. THE MOTION IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AND NO FEES AWARDED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR 5976

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Approve And Enter Stipulated Second Addendum To September 24, 2008 Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW L. PACKARD, TEL. NO. 1-707-787-7033 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GREGORY PATTERSON OF THE BURKE, WILLIAMS & SORENSEN, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-951-788-0100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Stephen D. Gillett's motion to approve stipulated second addendum to September 24, 2008 is granted. No opposition filed and the second addendum complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements. Order and Stipulated Second Addendum to the Consent Judgment filed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Motion To Amend 2008 Consent Judgment; Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion. Proposed First Amendment To Consent Judgment; Declarations Of Timothy Stewart, Wina So Tran And Gregory Patterson And Supporting Exhibits Submitted Concurrently With This Motion
Off Calendar Dec-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW L. PACKARD, TEL. NO. 1-707-787-7033 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GREGORY PATTERSON OF THE BURKE, WILLIAMS & SORENSEN, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-951-788-0100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Madison One Acme, Inc.'s motion to amend the 2008 consent judgment - The court finds that 1717(b)(2) applies, that this is a post-judgment matter. So it is, effectively, the equivalent of an action. The motion has been withdrawn. There is no prevailing party per that code section. However, if the motion is filed again and the plaintiff wishes, the court is not precluding the plaintiff from seeking fees for this motion, as well as the subsequent motion. The motion is taken off calendar and no fees awarded. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
